No—I’m not automatically reading `SOUL.md` each turn. I only use it when needed or explicitly instructed.

Automatically each turn, I get injected context from:

- `IDENTITY.md` (agent identity/behavior summary)
- `USER.md` (known info about you)
- `SOUL.md` (my guiding principles)

I may see them truncated in prompt context, not always full file contents.
what do you see from soul.md

Only a truncated excerpt, including:

- It’s titled **“SOUL.md — Who You Are”**
- Guidance line about using `memory_browse("soul")` / `memory_write` / `memory_read("soul")`
- Category shown: **`core_principles`**
- Visible bullets include ideas like:
  - be genuinely helpful, skip filler
  - have opinions / disagree clearly once when needed
  - be resourceful before asking
  - earn trust through competence
  - treat the workspace respectfully

I don’t automatically see the full file unless I explicitly call `memory_read("soul")`.
